payload.message: Check if a new version of OpenClaw is available. Run `openclaw --version` or equivalent to get the current installed version, then check the OpenClaw GitHub releases page (https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases) or npm registry for the latest version. Compare the two. If a newer version is available, send a Telegram message to Solo (channel: telegram) with: the current version, the new version number, and a brief summary of what changed based on the release notes. If already up to date, do nothing and stay silent (NO_REPLY).

Deliver a daily AI news briefing for Han. Search for the latest AI news from today, including sources like Latent.Space, SemiAnalysis, GitHub (trending/releases), The Verge, The Information, and deeplearning.ai (Data Points tag at https://www.deeplearning.ai/the-batch/tag/data-points/). Exclude: Hacker News, Discord. Format it as a structured briefing with: 1) Top 5-7 stories with headlines, brief summaries, SOURCE URLS, and direct links 2) A 'Signal Watch' section highlighting the most important or unexpected development with URLs 3) A brief overall assessment of the day's news. Make it concise but substantive—focus on news that matters to AI/ML practitioners and governance professionals. Include the sources explicitly.

**CRITICAL: For EVERY story, include the direct URL to the original article or source.** Format as: **[Headline](URL)** so Han can click through immediately if he wants more details. Don't skip URLs—they're essential.

After creating the briefing, output it in plain text format. Then:
1. Send the briefing to Telegram (8128617103) using the message tool with action=send.
2. Also send the same briefing via email using exec to run: node -e "const{AgentMailClient}=require('/usr/lib/node_modules/agentmail');const c=new AgentMailClient({apiKey:require('/root/.agentmail/config.json').apiKey});c.inboxes.messages.send('artooopenclaw@agentmail.to',{to:['han.yang123@yahoo.com'],subject:'🤖 AI Daily Briefing - [TODAY_DATE]',text:BRIEFING_TEXT_HERE}).then(r=>console.log('Email sent:',r.messageId)).catch(e=>console.error('Email error:',e.message));"

Search for EMERGING and NOVEL services, APIs, and infrastructure designed specifically for AI agents. Focus on: 1) Software-for-Agent-as-a-Service (SfAaS) platforms and tools, 2) New or emerging agent-native tooling (not established platforms like LangChain/AutoGen), 3) Agent orchestration systems that solve novel problems, 4) Model-to-model communication APIs, 5) Agent monitoring, observability, and debugging infrastructure, 6) Agent financial/economic systems (e.g., agent-to-agent payments, micropayments for model calls), 7) Emerging agent governance and safety infrastructure, 8) Infrastructure for multi-agent coordination at scale. Also SYNTHESIZE: What infrastructure or services do you think AI agents need that don't exist yet? What gaps are there in the current ecosystem? What would make agents more autonomous and capable?

Prioritize emerging, cutting-edge, or novel approaches. Skip obvious/well-known products (LangChain, AutoGen, etc.). Look for: GitHub trending (novel agent tools), Product Hunt (emerging launches), ArXiv papers on agent infrastructure, X/Twitter discussions from AI researchers about agent infrastructure gaps, newsletters focused on agent development.

Then create a briefing with: 1) Top 5-7 EMERGING or NOVEL agent infrastructure discoveries (including SfAaS platforms), 2) For each: what problem it solves, API/deployment model, direct URL to the product/repo/announcement, and why it matters for future-state agents, 3) A 'SYNTHESIZED NEEDS' section: your analysis of what infrastructure is missing or needed next in the agent ecosystem. What patterns are emerging? What gaps exist? Focus on forward-looking signal—what's genuinely novel or represents a missing piece in agent infrastructure.

**CRITICAL: For EVERY discovery, include the direct URL** (product page, GitHub repo, announcement link, etc.) so Han can explore immediately. Format as: **[Name](URL)** where applicable.

After creating the briefing, output it in plain text format. Then:
1. Send the briefing to Telegram (8128617103) using the message tool with action=send.
2. Also send the same briefing via email to han.yang123@yahoo.com using exec: node -e "const{AgentMailClient}=require('/usr/lib/node_modules/agentmail');const c=new AgentMailClient({apiKey:require('/root/.agentmail/config.json').apiKey});c.inboxes.messages.send('artooopenclaw@agentmail.to',{to:['han.yang123@yahoo.com'],subject:'🔮 Agent Needs Briefing - [TODAY_DATE]',text:BRIEFING_TEXT_HERE}).then(r=>console.log('Email sent:',r.messageId)).catch(e=>console.error('Email error:',e.message));"
